Analysis of hospitalized COVID-19 patients in South Africa revealed that individuals living with advanced HIV stages were significantly more susceptible to mortality related to the disease. 
 Failing to provide COVID-19 vaccines to countries with significant numbers of individuals living with uncontrolled advanced HIV poses an additional risk to those already vulnerable, potentially leading to new, concerning coronavirus variants. 
 Simultaneously addressing both diseases presents a more robust solution, allowing for stronger public health responses informed by insights gleaned from each challenge. Leveraging the lessons learned from combating COVID-19 alongside other emerging illnesses could significantly enhance disease prevention and control efforts.  In 2020, the World Health Organization (WHO) established a platform to standardize clinical data on individuals with suspected or confirmed cases of COVID-19.
 Individuals living with uncontrolled or advanced HIV experience a significantly increased risk of severe complications and death from COVID-19, with mortality rates approximately four times higher than those without the virus.  Numerous reports throughout the past year by organizations worldwide have confirmed this heightened vulnerability in those with weakened immune systems. These individuals may remain susceptible to SARS-CoV-2 infection for extended periods, ranging from weeks to months, highlighting the potential long-term impact of COVID-19 on their health. 
 As the global focus shifts from the immediate challenges of the COVID-19 pandemic towards long-term public health initiatives, it's crucial that governments, health organizations, research institutions, and other key stakeholders dedicate greater resources and attention to understanding the complex interplay between COVID-19 and HIV. 
 Leading researchers are uniting their efforts to address the dual public health crises of HIV and COVID-19 in Africa. Tulio de Oliveira, director of both the Centre for Epidemic Response and Innovation at Stellenbosch University and the KwaZulu-Natal Research Innovation and Sequencing Platform, exemplifies this commitment.  
 Individuals with HIV face significantly increased risk of mortality from COVID-19.  A population-based analysis encompassing approximately 3.5 million individuals from the Western Cape Province of South Africa revealed a stark correlation: those living with HIV were double the likelihood of succumbing to COVID-19 related deaths compared to their uninfected counterparts. 
 Pre-pandemic research indicates that individuals living with HIV experience a significantly higher risk of death due to COVID-19 infections. Studies have shown that those with HIV face a 3050% increased mortality rate compared to the general population during the COVID-19 pandemic. 
 A delayed response to the pandemic, particularly in nations grappling with uncontrolled HIV transmission, poses a significant risk of viral mutations. Such unchecked virus evolution could result in variants of the SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus that are highly contagious and significantly diminish the effectiveness of available vaccines. 
 Individuals within this population have significantly suppressed immune systems, characterized by a blood cell count of less than 200 CD4 T cells per microliter. This is a drastic reduction compared to the typical range of 500-1500 cells per microliter found in individuals without HIV infection.  
 Tragically, we've lost several colleagues, including prominent scientists and healthcare experts, to the pandemic.  Furthermore, the increased difficulty accessing life-saving treatment and ongoing care for individuals living with HIV has exacerbated existing health challenges. Consequently, more people have been hospitalized due to severe conditions like tuberculosis and cryptococcal meningitis. 
 While healthy individuals typically recover from infections within two weeks, patients undergoing cancer chemotherapy and other immunosuppressive treatments often experience prolonged infections.  
 Individuals living with advanced, uncontrolled HIV have also exhibited these characteristics. 
 Preliminary analysis of data from 37 countries, primarily from South Africa, found a striking correlation: individuals living with HIV were 30% more likely to succumb to COVID-19 after hospitalization, even when accounting for other contributing factors. 
 Across South Africa, healthcare professionals witness the harrowing effects of COVID-19 firsthand.  We see the devastating impact it has had on our nation, as patients struggle, and resources dwindle.

"It's not a coincidence that we're seeing an uptick in cases now," stated Dr. Bill Powderly, co-director of the infectious-disease division at Washington University's School of Medicine in St. Louis.  He specifically noted this timing coincides with expectations for increased transmission following the July Fourth weekend. 
 Simultaneously, certain regions grapple with significant vaccine hesitancy, contributing to the rise of deeply concerning vaccine resistance.  Furthermore, the highly transmissible variant originating in India has rapidly increased its prevalence within the infection landscape. 
 Over half of Americans have received at least one dose of the COVID-19 vaccine, with data from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention showing a national rate of 55.6%. However, five states stand out as having experienced significant increases in case rates per capita within just two weeks, despite lower vaccination rates: Missouri (45.9%), Arkansas (43%), Nevada (50.9%), Louisiana (39.2%), and Utah (49.5%). 
 Johns Hopkins University data reveals a significant surge in COVID-19 cases.  Daily confirmed infections averaged approximately 23,600 on Monday, marking a substantial increase from the previous average of 11,300 on June 23rd. Notably, all states except Maine and South Dakota have seen their case numbers rise over the past two weeks. 
 The US COVID-19 caseload is surging once more, doubling in just three weeks due to the highly contagious Delta variant. Contributing factors include slower vaccination efforts and large Fourth of July celebrations that likely facilitated increased transmission.

A Brazilian Senate committee recently released a comprehensive 1,100-page report detailing the consequences of President Bolsonaro's disregard for human rights amidst a global pandemic.  The report offers a stark glimpse into the challenges faced as a nation's leader prioritizes policies over the well-being of its people during an unprecedented crisis. 
 The committee’s report, born from months of hearings and investigations, paints a stark picture: President [Name] prioritized ideology over science during the COVID-19 pandemic, jeopardizing the health and safety of Brazilians. 
 Bolsonaro's mishandling of Brazil's COVID-19 pandemic has had devastating consequences, leaving the nation grappling with a profound public health crisis and mounting economic losses.  President Jair Bolsonaro's controversial response to the virus, marked by downplaying its severity, promotion of unverified cures, and prioritizing political agendas over public health measures, has fueled a climate of misinformation and delayed vital healthcare interventions. This reckless approach has left Brazil facing a steep climb towards recovery, one marred by preventable deaths and widespread economic hardship. 
 The report found his ultimate goal was to achieve rapid disease spread in order to reach herd immunity in Brazil.  In addition to disregarding World Health Organization recommendations designed to safeguard public health, he actively hindered both Congress and local authorities from adhering to established guidelines. 
 In late 2020, local officials alerted the federal government to a looming oxygen shortage, yet instead of procuring supplies, the Bolsonaro administration diverted resources to distribute ineffective medications unrelated to combating the virus. This alarming decision was revealed in a recent report. 
 The Bolsonaro administration failed to engage with vaccine offers made by the Butantan Institute, a research center under the São Paulo state government's control. The report further revealed that there is evidence of potential corruption within a specific vaccine procurement contract that was allegedly presented to President Bolsonaro during the investigation. 
 According to a witness account, Jair Bolsonaro had pledged to file a formal report with the federal police regarding this incident. However, no such report was filed, according to the record. The report itself subsequently recommended various criminal charges against Bolsonaro and other government officials, along with his potential impeachment. 
 The Attorney General, appointed by President Bolsonaro, must thoroughly consider the substantial evidence presented to the committee.  Given the widespread criticism regarding alleged favoritism towards the President in their decision-making, a serious examination of this evidence is imperative. 
 As oxygen supply dwindled, dozens of COVID-19 patients perished.  An investigative committee revealed that the government had deployed unproven medications to Indigenous populations despite their lack of efficacy. Only after repeated demands from the Supreme Court did the government draft a plan to protect Indigenous communities from the pandemic. 
 In July 2020, he vetoed laws requiring mask mandates for churches and overcrowded Brazilian prisons. This was exemplified by a particularly tragic incident in Amazonas state during January 2021. 
 The COVID-19 pandemic has claimed over 600,000 lives in Brazil, according to reports that highlight the tragic potential for preventable deaths.  Evidence reveals thousands of those losses could have been avoided. Notably, President Jair Bolsonaro's actions fueled the spread of misinformation about the virus, actively hindered social distancing measures, and consistently disregarded the use of masks. His habit of shaking hands with large crowds of supporters further contributed to a surge in infections, demonstrating his disregard for public health during this critical period.

Since assuming office on January 20th, we've seen a 22% increase in supply availability. Our commitment is to maintain this minimum level for the next three weeks. We will also continue working closely with manufacturers to expedite production and boost supply levels further. 
 The President announced today that the administration will boost vaccine supply across the nation. To address continued efforts to combat the pandemic, the Biden-Harris Administration is expanding weekly vaccine availability, increasing the total number of doses delivered to states, Tribes, and territories to 10.5 million nationwide beginning this week. 
 This program leverages the combined expertise of 21 national pharmacy partners and networks of independent pharmacies, encompassing over 40,000 retail locations across the nation (see list below). It is a cornerstone of the Administration's National Strategy for equitable vaccine access to the American public. 
 Phase one of this program will distribute a limited supply of vaccines to select retail pharmacies across the nation, enabling them to vaccinate prioritized populations free of charge. 
 President Biden announced expanded vaccine supplies, the launch of the Federal Retail Pharmacy Program, and an increase in FEMA reimbursements for states, tribal governments, and territories today,  a crucial step in combating the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. 

States, tribes, and territories will receive a 5 percent increase in vaccine supply this week, bolstering access to vaccinations for Americans. Additionally, the Federal Retail Pharmacy Program will streamline vaccine distribution by enabling pharmacies across the nation to provide convenient and accessible vaccination services. With over 26 million confirmed COVID-19 cases in the U.S., President Biden's comprehensive National Strategy to fight the pandemic is gaining momentum with this expansion of resources.  
 The Biden-Harris administration is launching the first phase of the Federal Retail Pharmacy Program for COVID-19 vaccination. Starting February 11th, individuals eligible for the vaccine will have access to vaccines at selected pharmacies nationwide. This program aims to expand access to COVID-19 vaccines through a network of participating retailers.  
 These new steps will accelerate vaccine administration, supporting the President's goal of delivering 100 million doses within 100 days, and expanding access to vaccines for millions more Americans in the coming weeks. 
 The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) collaborated with state governments to identify initial pharmacy partners, prioritizing those who could effectively serve vulnerable populations disproportionately impacted by severe COVID-19 illness. 
 To increase vaccine access, we must prioritize expanding vaccine supply to state, tribal, and territorial health agencies, as well as enhancing funding for local public health initiatives to support widespread vaccination programs. 
 Next week, the President announced the launch of the first phase of the federal pharmacy program, with participating pharmacies nationwide beginning to offer vaccinations to their communities. 
 Individuals can check their local pharmacy's website to see if COVID-19 vaccines are currently available, as supplies will be limited during the initial rollout phase.  For more detailed information on vaccine availability and distribution, please visit cdc.gov/covid19.

The Delta variant is rapidly spreading throughout the country, including New York State.  Daily confirmed cases have surged by over 1,000% in just six weeks, with over 80% of new infections linked to this specific variant. 
 We must recommit our efforts to contain the spread of this variant. Healthcare heroes have been instrumental in battling the virus, and now they must rally to support the vital fight between vaccination and the new threat posed by the variant. 
 I advocate for private businesses to adopt vaccinated-only admission policies, and I believe that school districts should mandate vaccinations for their teachers.  
 Legal mandates are essential for both the implementation of vaccination policies in private businesses and in local school districts. Without such mandates, neither action is likely to occur. Businesses will only enforce vaccine requirements if they are mandated by law, and local schools will face significant challenges in making these decisions without clear legal guidance. 
 Governor Cuomo has mandated that all healthcare workers in New York State receive COVID-19 vaccinations by Monday, September 27th. This mandate includes staff at hospitals, long-term care facilities (including nursing homes, adult care centers, and other congregate settings).  
 The State Department of Health will issue Section 16 orders, mandating that hospitals, long-term care facilities (LTCFs), and nursing homes create and adhere to vaccination policies for their employees. These policies will allow for some exemptions based on religious or medical reasons, with limitations.  
 Eligible New Yorkers who have completed their two-dose COVID-19 vaccine series are now eligible for a third dose, which can be received up to 28 days after their second dose. This change is effective immediately. 
 The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) advises that people with moderately to severely compromised immunity receive an additional COVID-19 vaccination dose. This includes individuals who: 

* Have been receiving active cancer treatments, such as those targeting tumors or blood cancers;
* Have received an organ transplant and are taking immunosuppressive medications;
* Have undergone a stem cell transplant within the past two years, or are currently taking immunosuppressant medications; 
* Suffer from moderate or severe primary immunodeficiency, like DiGeorge syndrome or Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome;
* Have advanced or untreated HIV infection;
* Are undergoing treatment with high-dose corticosteroids, cancer chemotherapy that severely weakens the immune system, or other medications known to suppress immune response. 
 Guided by scientific evidence, we continue to prioritize action today through the recommendations of Dr. Zucker and our esteemed federal and state health experts. However, further steps are necessary to ensure comprehensive progress. 
 Lieutenant Governor Kathy Hochul's administration was informed of the impending announcement beforehand.  Last year when COVID-19 hit New York, residents took action, while federal officials remained dismissive, as Governor Cuomo pointed out. 
 Governor Cuomo announced that New Yorkers with compromised immune systems will now be eligible for a third COVID-19 vaccine dose, in line with the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention’s recent recommendation. This authorization was made by the Department of Health. 
 Over 75% of hospital workers in the state (representing roughly 450,000 individuals), 74% of adult care facility workers (comprising 30,000 employees), and 68% of nursing home staff (making up 145,500 people) have completed their vaccination series.

Novavax, based in Gaithersburg, Maryland, developed the investigational COVID-19 vaccine and spearheaded the PREVENT-19 clinical trial.

 The PREVENT-19 study aimed to determine whether NVX-CoV2373 could prevent symptomatic COVID-19 disease at least seven days after the second dose compared to a placebo.  Data shared today is based on 77 confirmed cases of COVID-19 in trial participants observed between January 25th and April 30th, 2021. 
 The candidate demonstrated 100% protection against moderate and severe COVID-19 disease. For individuals at high risk of developing complications from COVID-19, such as those aged 65 or older and those under 65 with certain comorbidities or frequent exposure to the virus, the vaccine showed an efficacy rate of 91%. 
 Random assignment within the study yielded a 2:1 ratio of participants receiving NVX-CoV2373 and those receiving placebo.  Due to the blinding process employed in the trial, neither the investigators nor the participants were able to discern which treatment they received, ensuring an unbiased assessment of the vaccine's effects.
 The safety data for the investigational vaccine showed generally favorable tolerability.  Participants reported mild to moderate injection site pain and tenderness as the most frequent local side effects. Systemic effects included fatigue, headaches, and muscle aches that typically lasted less than two days. 
 Sixty-three COVID-19 cases were reported among the roughly 10,000 participants receiving a placebo, compared to fourteen cases among the approximately 20,000 individuals receiving the investigational vaccine.  Of those cases in the placebo group, ten were categorized as moderate and four as severe. 
 There were no reports of moderate or severe adverse events in the investigational vaccine group. 
 The PREVENT-19 trial commenced in late December 2020, enrolling adult volunteers at 119 study sites across the NIAID-supported COVID-19 Prevention Network (CoVPN).  Participants were randomly assigned to receive two doses of either an investigational vaccine or a placebo solution, given 21 days apart.  
 U.S. clinical trials have confirmed that the Novavax vaccine is both safe and highly effective against COVID-19.  Results from a Phase 3 trial involving 29,960 adult volunteers in the U.S. and Mexico revealed that the investigational vaccine, NVX-CoV2373, achieved an impressive 90.4% efficacy rate in preventing symptomatic COVID-19 infection.

On Friday, October 22, 2021, at 13:00 to 17:05 Geneva time (CEST), the ninth meeting of the World Health Organization's (WHO) Emergency Committee regarding the COVID-19 pandemic took place. The meeting convened under the International Health Regulations (2005). 
 Two members reported changes to their professional responsibilities since our last meeting.  Upon review, these changes did not create a potential conflict of interest and were approved for continued participation in the Committee. One advisor was selected for appointment by the WHO and will no longer participate in the Committee in his previous capacity. 
 He emphasized the critical role of the Committee's efforts, recognizing the significant challenges presented by the COVID-19 pandemic. 
 All members and advisors were informed of their obligation to promptly disclose any relevant interests—personal, professional, financial, intellectual, or commercial—to WHO that could potentially create a conflict of interest, either directly or through perception. 
 The Director-General underscored the critical need for a targeted, risk-based approach to vaccination alongside effective public health and social measures (PHSM).  He reiterated the global call for action to accelerate vaccination efforts and achieve 40% coverage for all populations by year's end of 2021. 
 There were no identified conflicts of interest.  Following a brief opening by Professor Didier Houssin, who took the lead in reviewing the meeting's objectives and agenda, the session commenced. 
 Legal Counsel and Compliance, Risk Management, and Ethics representatives gave a briefing to attendees outlining their roles and responsibilities.  The Ethics Officer from the Department of Compliance, Risk Management, and Ethics presented an overview of the World Health Organization's (WHO) declaration of interests process for members and advisors. 
 The Committee discussed critical topics, including SARS-CoV-2 surveillance strategies, identifying challenges and barriers; understanding immunity gained from natural infection or vaccination, examining the protection each method provides; evaluating intra-action reviews' efficacy in assisting States Parties to refine their response efforts; and emphasizing the importance of maintaining a comprehensive and adaptable Public Health Security Model (PHSM) grounded in informed risk assessments. 
 A reminder was given to all members regarding the confidentiality of meeting discussions and the committee's work.  Each participant was then reviewed. 
 The Committee raised concerns regarding the challenges faced by the African Region in addressing the COVID-19 pandemic.  These challenges included difficulties in accessing sufficient vaccines, diagnostic tools, and therapeutics. The committee also highlighted the need for effective data collection, analysis, and reporting on epidemiological and quality laboratory data including genomic sequencing data to facilitate monitoring of the pandemic's evolution. 
 The Committee advocated for robust funding, personnel training, and technological assistance to empower the region and its member states in enhancing their surveillance and response capabilities. 
 The Secretariat provided updates on several key issues related to the ongoing pandemic: 

* Global epidemiological trends, highlighting transmission drivers and factors contributing to the spread of COVID-19. 
* Projections on the anticipated evolution of the virus, encompassing potential future scenarios. 
* The continued challenge of global vaccine inequity, with projected increases in COVAX Facility vaccine supply.
* States Parties' growing adherence to recommendations regarding health measures for international travel, demonstrating increased consistency.

 Over six million remaining doses will be distributed directly to countries facing surges in cases, nations impacted by crises, and to our partners and neighbors such as Canada, Mexico, India, and the Republic of Korea. 
 We will continue to base our decisions on science while working closely with our democratic partners to coordinate a global response, especially through initiatives like the G7. 
 Our goal in sharing these doses is not to seek favors or bargain for concessions. We share these vaccines to save lives and to pave the way toward eradicating the pandemic – leading the world by demonstrating our commitment to global health and our unwavering values. 
 "Our commitment to vaccinating every eligible American against COVID-19 remains strong, but as we strive for a brighter future here at home, we must recognize that ending the pandemic requires global action.  It's about ensuring all nations have access to vaccines and tools to combat this disease." 
 The United States has already invested $4 billion in COVAX, a global initiative designed to ensure equitable access to COVID-19 vaccines.  We are also actively building partnerships to strengthen global vaccine manufacturing capacity. 
 My administration believes waiving intellectual property protections for COVID-19 vaccines is necessary to accelerate vaccine production and equitable distribution.  A broader global supply chain will ultimately ensure more people have access to safe, effective vaccines. 
 As long as the pandemic continues to pose a threat globally, Americans remain susceptible. However, the United States remains steadfastly committed to ensuring international vaccination efforts receive the same urgency and dedication we've shown domestically.

California has been a driving force in battling COVID-19, prioritizing robust vaccination efforts guided by scientific research and real-world data. These measures have significantly curbed the virus's spread and saved countless lives, particularly those in our most susceptible populations, as Governor Newsom highlighted. 
 Booster shots are now available to individuals aged 16 and older who received the Pfizer vaccine at least six months ago, those aged 18 and up who received the Moderna vaccine at least six months prior, and anyone aged 18 or older who received the Johnson & Johnson vaccine at least two months ago. 
 To commemorate the one-year anniversary of the first COVID-19 vaccine administered in California, Governor Gavin Newsom released a new column titled "On the Record." This update emphasizes California's innovative approach to combatting the virus, including its successful efforts to slow transmission rates and bolster immunity through boosters and vaccinations. As winter approaches, the governor urges Californians to prioritize continued protection against COVID-19 while striving for long-term immunity. 
 In light of rising COVID-19 case rates following Thanksgiving, California Governor Newsom strongly recommended that all eligible residents get vaccinated and boosted as the most effective way to safeguard themselves and mitigate further transmission of the virus. 
 California leads the nation in COVID-19 vaccine distribution, delivering over 62.7 million doses to residents.  With 86% of eligible Californians receiving at least one dose and more than 6 million adults having received a booster shot, California has effectively immunized its population against the virus. 
 We must prioritize maintaining a robust immune system—not just for ourselves, but for the well-being of our entire communities. Californians have consistently shown incredible resilience and determination, facing each challenge with vigor and courage. Together, we will continue to be at the forefront of combating COVID-19 across the nation.

The COVID-19 pandemic stands as the deadliest health crisis in U.S. history, claiming a toll far exceeding that of the 1918 Spanish Flu. 
 While it's debatable whether the COVID-19 pandemic will claim the deadliest title in US history, its current death toll has undoubtedly surpassed that of the Civil War.  The widely accepted figure for the Civil War's fatalities is 620,000, and this pandemic has already overtaken that number. 
 The number of COVID-19 deaths in the United States is significantly higher than expected, accounting for roughly 14% of the nearly 4.7 million reported fatalities globally. This disproportionately high death toll is concerning given that the US only comprises around 4.2% of the global population. 
 Over 675,400 Covid-19 deaths have been recorded by STAT's tracker.  This staggering number is highlighted by Howard Markel, director of the Center for the History of Medicine at the University of Michigan School of Public Health.  He notes that these figures represent a significant loss of life. 
 "It's widely acknowledged that the US struggled with initial pandemic control efforts," asserted Dr. Morens, an expert whose work encompasses both the 1918 flu and contemporary COVID-19 research. 
 Markel highlighted the significant impact the pandemic had on the U.S., attributing it to the country’s inadequate response in the early stages.  Agreeing with this assessment, David Morens, a medical historian at the National Institutes of Allergy and Infectious Diseases, underscored the need for stronger preparedness measures. 
 In 2011, historian David Hacker of Binghamton University in New York State challenged prevailing estimates of the Civil War's death toll by proposing a revised figure closer to 750,000 deaths.  He published his findings in the journal *Civil War History*. 
 Despite significant progress in vaccination efforts, COVID-19 mortality rates are still lagging behind the impact of the 1918 Spanish flu pandemic.  Specifically, the percentage of total national population affected by Spanish Flu death is currently higher than that for COVID-19. 
 The United States is among the hardest-hit nations globally in terms of death rates from influenza pandemics due to our aging population and high mortality rate. This observation was made by Cecile Viboud, an infectious diseases epidemiologist renowned for her research on deaths from the 1918 flu pandemic. Additionally, European countries experienced significant hardship in this regard. 
 Despite the devastating loss of life during the Spanish flu pandemic in 1918, advancements in modern medicine have significantly mitigated its long-term effects. While the cumulative years lost due to the virus remain a stark reminder of its immense impact, contemporary medical technology offers tools and knowledge that were unavailable then. 
 Comparing events spanning over a century presents unique challenges.  For example, the United States population in 1918 was only a fraction of its current size. 
 For individuals suffering severe COVID-19 lung damage, medical professionals utilize ventilators or extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) devices. These machines provide oxygen directly into the bloodstream when a patient's heart and lungs are unable to function effectively. 
 The 1918 Spanish flu pandemic inflicted the deadliest blow on American life, with estimates from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention suggesting a staggering loss of 675,000 lives within its two-year rampage across the nation.  
 "The average age of those who perished during the 1918 flu pandemic was 28 years old, while COVID-related deaths predominantly affect the elderly population, as per Dr. Viboud, a researcher at the National Institutes of Health's Fogerty International Center."  
 Vaccines offering high levels of protection against severe COVID-19 complications were available long before 1918. Despite this, we've continued to see alarming numbers of deaths from the disease, although they have been occurring at a slower pace compared to the early stages of the pandemic. 
 The COVID-19 pandemic has surpassed the 1918 Spanish flu as the deadliest disease in American history, leaving a tragic mark on our nation's health.  Volunteers from the American Red Cross played a crucial role in tending to patients suffering from influenza during the 1918 pandemic. A poignant reminder of the devastating impact of infectious diseases, their efforts provide insight into the challenges faced during this era.

The assessment considers both the urgency of the emergency and the potential benefits and drawbacks of using a particular product to address it, ultimately weighing these factors to determine the most suitable course of action. 
 Research on pregnant women will be conducted to advance understanding of this drug's impact and efficacy.  The World Health Organization (WHO) Emergency Use Listing (EUL) process evaluates the appropriateness of novel medical products for use in public health emergencies. 
 The World Health Organization's (WHO) Emergency Use Listing (EUL) procedure evaluates the quality, safety, and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines, serving as a crucial prerequisite for global vaccine distribution through COVAX. The EUL process also facilitates expedited regulatory approvals for countries seeking to import and administer these vaccines within their borders. 
 “This expanded emergency use listing significantly increases vaccine availability,” stated Dr. Mariângela Simão, WHO Assistant-Director-General for Access to Medicines and Health Products.  "Vaccines are the most effective medical tools we have to end this pandemic." 
 The World Health Organization (WHO) has granted Emergency Use Listing (EUL) to COVAXIN®, developed by Bharat Biotech, marking the eighth COVID-19 vaccine to receive this designation. This latest addition strengthens the WHO's growing portfolio of validated vaccines for combating SARS-CoV-2, which causes COVID-19. 
 COVAXIN®, a COVID-19 vaccine, was reviewed by the World Health Organization's Strategic Advisory Group of Experts on Immunization (SAGE) on October 5th. SAGE provides recommendations and policies regarding the use of vaccines in various populations. 
 Available data on the vaccine's safety and effectiveness during pregnancy from clinical trials are limited. However, preliminary studies have presented encouraging results. Over 120,000 pregnant women in India have received the vaccine with no reported short-term adverse effects. 
 The priority is to deliver life-saving medications, vaccines, and diagnostic tools swiftly to combat this emergency, all while ensuring they meet rigorous standards for safety, effectiveness, and quality. 
 COVAXIN® underwent evaluation by the World Health Organization (WHO) under their Essential Use Listing (EUL) procedure. This involved reviewing data related to quality, safety, effectiveness, a risk management plan, and program suitability. 
 The World Health Organization's Technical Advisory Group (TAG), comprised of global regulatory experts, has confirmed that the vaccine meets WHO safety and efficacy standards for COVID-19 protection. This assessment indicates a significant net benefit from vaccination, exceeding associated risks, allowing for its use globally. 
 The COVID-19 vaccine is a single-dose formulation containing an inactivated SARS-CoV-2 antigen, available in both single-dose vials (for immediate use) and multi-dose vials with five, ten, or twenty dose options.  
 COVAXIN® demonstrated an impressive 78% effectiveness against COVID-19, regardless of severity, 14 or more days following the second dose. Its ease of storage makes it particularly well-suited for use in low- and middle-income countries around the world. 
 The SAGE committee recommends administering the vaccine in two doses, with an interval of four weeks between each dose for individuals aged 18 or older. This recommendation also addresses specific population subgroups such as pregnant and lactating women.  
 We must sustain our efforts to ensure equitable vaccine distribution, prioritizing those most in need, particularly those who haven't yet received their first doses.  Only then can we truly celebrate a breakthrough.

Phase 2 encompasses individuals working in settings impacted by significant COVID-19 case clusters and whose roles are essential for maintaining the functionality of core state services. 
 Eligibility for COVID-19 vaccinations under the state's Phase 3 plan will be determined by both age and facility affiliation.  Phase 3 encompasses residents and staff at congregate living facilities such as college dorms, group homes, and shelters, along with individuals within the corrections system.

TDH anticipates receiving approximately 311,000 new doses by week of March 22nd, and an additional 30% boost in weekly supplies with the Johnson & Johnson allocation expected week of March 29th.  

Tennessee Health Commissioner Lisa Piercey, MD, MBA, FAAP stated, "As vaccine supplies have increased, we've expanded our COVID-19 eligibility criteria in line with our commitment to protect those most vulnerable to serious illness and death from the virus."   
 Tennessee's COVID-19 vaccine eligibility is expanding significantly. Starting March 18th, Tennesseans aged 55 and older, as well as those in Phase 2 of the state’s vaccination plan, can receive their shots. Governor Lee announced this expansion today through a video message. By April 5th, all Tennessee residents age 16 or older will be eligible for COVID-19 vaccinations. 
 Phase 3 vaccine distribution prioritizes grocery store workers who didn't initially qualify for vaccination due to age or other risk factors. To guarantee they receive access, direct vaccine allocation to these stores is being implemented.  
 These groups encompass a range of essential workers: child welfare and adult protective services agency staff working directly with the public; commercial food manufacturing employees; agricultural workers involved in food production and supply chain safety; correctional system workers excluded from Phase 1a1; public transit drivers and maintenance personnel; and individuals employed in transportation, public infrastructure, telecommunications, and utility sectors. 
 All Tennessee adults will be eligible for COVID-19 vaccination by April 5th, as announced today by Governor Bill Lee of Tennessee. 
 Tennessee is experiencing a surge in the availability of COVID-19 vaccines, allowing for swift expansion of eligibility to receive vaccinations.

The FDA, after a comprehensive review of all available scientific evidence, concluded that Evusheld shows promise as a preventative measure before exposure to the virus in certain adults and adolescents aged twelve and older who weigh at least 40 kilograms. 
 Patients should consult with their healthcare provider to determine if Evusheld is an appropriate pre-exposure prevention option for them. It's important to note that Evusheld is not a replacement for vaccination, and it shouldn't be used instead of recommended vaccinations for those eligible for COVID-19 vaccines. 
 This authorization grants permission to utilize a combination of two monoclonal antibodies for reducing the risk of contracting COVID-19 in eligible patients. 
 This product is authorized for use by healthy individuals who have not tested positive for, or had recent exposure to, someone who has tested positive for SARS-CoV-2. 
 The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has authorized several vaccines to combat COVID-19, both for preventing illness altogether and for mitigating severe consequences like hospitalization and death.  They strongly encourage eligible individuals to receive these vaccines. 
 Evusheld remains the currently authorized and most effective option for pre-exposure prophylaxis against COVID-19 in eligible populations.  
 Tixagevimab and cilgavimab are long-acting monoclonal antibodies designed to target the spike protein of SARS-CoV-2. These medications work by blocking the virus's ability to attach and enter human cells.  Importantly, these antibodies bind to distinct areas on the spike protein that don't overlap, enhancing their effectiveness. 
 The Emergency Use Authorization (EUA) process differs from traditional FDA approval.  When considering an EUA, the FDA weighs all available scientific evidence to ensure a comprehensive assessment. This involves meticulously evaluating both potential risks and benefits associated with the product to make an informed decision. 
 Explore FDA-approved or authorized COVID-19 vaccines and learn how monoclonal antibodies work. These lab-created proteins mimic the immune system's defense against harmful pathogens, including viruses. 
 Individuals may be eligible for authorization if they have either a compromised immune system due to a medical condition or are on immunosuppressant medications.  These individuals might not mount a sufficient immune response to a COVID-19 vaccination (see fact sheet for healthcare providers). Alternatively, those with a history of severe adverse reactions to a COVID-19 vaccine and/or its components should avoid vaccination according to the approved or authorized schedule. 
 Vaccines remain our most effective defense against COVID-19. 
 The agency has concluded that the known and potential benefits of Evusheld, when utilized in accordance with the authorization's terms and conditions, outweigh any known or potential risks associated with the product. 
 The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has authorized a new long-acting monoclonal antibody treatment for pre-exposure prevention of COVID-19 in certain individuals.  Today's authorization grants emergency use authorization (EUA) to AstraZeneca's Evusheld, which consists of tixagevimab co-packaged with cilgavimab and administered together. This treatment is now approved for adults and adolescents aged 12 years and older weighing at least 40 kilograms (about 88 pounds).  
 Evusheld can provide up to six months of protection against COVID-19 through a single dose administered in two separate intramuscular injections (one injection per antibody, given immediately after the other).  However, it is not approved for treating COVID-19 or preventing exposure to the virus after it has occurred. 
 Individuals with compromised immune systems may not mount a sufficient immune response to COVID-19 vaccination, while those who have experienced severe adverse reactions to previous COVID-19 vaccines cannot receive one and require alternative preventative measures.  Dr. Patrizia Cavazzoni, Director of the FDA's Center for Drug Evaluation and Research, made this statement regarding vaccine options.

COVID-19 infections were linked to an elevated risk for several serious complications, including pericarditis (inflammation of the surrounding tissue of the heart), irregular heart rhythms (arrhythmias), heart attacks, strokes, pulmonary embolisms, deep vein thrombosis, acute kidney injury, and more. 
 Data analyzed for this study originated from Clalit Health Services, a leading healthcare organization in Israel and one of the largest integrated health record databases worldwide.  This represents the largest peer-reviewed evaluation of the safety of a COVID-19 vaccine administered within a large-scale national vaccination effort. 
 This real-world study confirms, complements, and expands upon the results of the Pfizer/BioNTech phase 3 randomized clinical trial, involving 21,720 participants.  Researchers observed these findings to be consistent with previous reports. 
 Read the latest HMS news here! A recent study published August 25th in The New England Journal of Medicine by researchers from Clalit Research Institute in Israel, Harvard Medical School, and the Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health has been making waves.  
 The Chan School of Public Health provides a comprehensive answer to that question. Their research, which assessed the safety of the Pfizer/BioNTech BNT162b2 mRNA vaccine, was based on analysis of data from nearly 2 million individuals in Israel. This represents one of the largest real-world assessments of COVID-19 vaccine safety to date. 
 This novel analysis provides a deeper understanding of the vaccine's safety by encompassing extensive data and delivering a more thorough evaluation of potential side effects across various outcomes. 
 Clinical trials over the past year have shown that COVID-19 vaccines are effective at preventing severe illness, hospitalization, and death. 
 This comprehensive epidemiological study offers valuable insights into vaccine safety, which could help individuals make informed decisions about vaccination, Dr. Reis stated. 
 Israel's nationwide COVID-19 vaccination campaign offered the Clalit Research Institute an invaluable opportunity to assess vaccine safety in real-world settings.  The institute leveraged its vast digital datasets, a resource rich with comprehensive information, to analyze side effects without relying on self-reported cases. Ran Balicer, Director of the Clalit Research Institute and Chief Innovation Officer for Clalit, emphasizes that this approach offers a unique advantage for evaluating vaccine effectiveness in a natural environment. 
 Despite occasional reports of rare side effects, a critical question remains:  how safe are COVID-19 vaccines in everyday life beyond controlled clinical trials? 
 Myocarditis, or inflammation of the heart muscle, was a rare condition observed at a rate of 2.7 excess cases per 100,000 vaccinated people. This signifies that for every 100,000 individuals, there were approximately 2.7 more cases of myocarditis than expected based on pre-vaccination rates, as researchers indicated. 
 This study compared adverse events reported by vaccinated and unvaccinated individuals, as well as those who contracted SARS-CoV-2 infection and those who remained uninfected.  The analysis revealed a low incidence of adverse events following vaccination. 
 "A key driver of vaccine hesitancy has been a lack of information about potential side effects," explains Ben Reis, HMS assistant professor of pediatrics at Boston Children's Hospital and director of the predictive medicine group in the Computational Health Informatics Program at Boston Children's. 
 Individuals hesitant to get vaccinated due to concerns about extremely rare side effects like myocarditis may be unaware of the actual risk involved.  Reis emphasized that the risk of developing myocarditis is significantly higher in those who remain unvaccinated and subsequently become infected with the virus, highlighting a crucial point in the vaccination debate. 
 The researchers concluded that these findings confirm the overall safety of the vaccine and emphasize that the risk of experiencing severe complications from SARS-CoV-2 infection is significantly higher than any potential vaccine side effects. 
 These results demonstrate the remarkable safety of this mRNA vaccine, significantly outpacing the risks associated with contracting COVID-19 naturally. As Dr. Balicer has pointed out, these alternative outcomes pose a significantly greater and more frequent threat of severe side effects. 
 Uninfected, unvaccinated individuals presented a significantly lower risk of myocarditis compared to those who were both infected and not vaccinated. Specifically, the rate of myocarditis was estimated at 11 excess cases per 100,000 people among those with both infection and lack of vaccination.

Genomic scientists at a press conference revealed a concerning new variant features an unusually high mutation count, exceeding 30 mutations within its key spike protein. This protein plays a crucial role in how the virus enters human cells. 
 Scientists like Peacock, de Oliveira, Ferguson, Jha have cautioned that determining the full impact of these mutations on vaccine effectiveness is premature.  
 Officials cautioned that the mutation might lead to immune evasion and increased viral transmissibility, but stressed the need for further data to determine its potential impact on vaccine effectiveness. 
 Javid stated that the UK has yet to detect any cases of the new variant in its borders. This B.1.1.529 variant was categorized as a "variant under monitoring" by the World Health Organization, which is expected to receive detailed briefings from South African officials on Friday. 
 The Omicron variant has been detected in South Africa, Botswana, and a traveller from South Africa to Hong Kong, Dr. Phaahla confirmed. 
 He emphasized the continued need for laboratory research to investigate vaccine and antibody evasion strategies.  News of a new variant identified by South African health officials prompted immediate reactions, including the UK's decision to temporarily ban flights from six African countries. 
 The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) is actively investigating a new COVID-19 variant circulating, particularly in South Africa.  While further data is required for a complete analysis, Health Secretary Sajid Javid confirmed they are taking preemptive measures as the variant's potential for increased transmissibility compared to the Delta strain has been identified. Javid also expressed concern that current vaccines may offer less protection against this new variant. 
 UK Health Minister Sajid Javid announced that flights from South Africa, Namibia, Lesotho, Botswana, Eswatini, and Zimbabwe would be suspended as of midday Friday. All six countries will now be on the UK's red list, requiring all arriving residents and British and Irish nationals to undergo a 10-day hotel quarantine at their own expense. 
 "The new COVID-19 variant circulating in South Africa exhibits significantly higher mutation rates than anticipated," stated Tulio de Oliveira, Director of the Center for Epidemic Response and Innovation. "This variant is spreading rapidly, leading to concerns about increased pressure on our healthcare systems within the coming days and weeks." 
 "Initially, we saw some clusters, but yesterday, our scientists from the Network of Genomic Surveillance alerted us to a new variant, identified as Joe Phaahla," stated South Africa's Minister of Health, on Thursday. "However, the exact origin of this variant remains unclear." 
 Despite relatively few new infections nationwide, currently hovering below 5% positivity rates, public health officials anticipate a potential fourth COVID-19 surge due to sluggish vaccination efforts.  
 "Further research is needed to fully evaluate the clinical severity of this specific variant compared to past ones," officials stated.  "The true impact of this variant remains unclear, and our most effective defense against its potential for harm remains vaccination." 
 A concerning new COVID-19 variant has emerged, potentially evading immunity and exhibiting increased transmission rates, according to South African scientists.  South Africa's health minister revealed the discovery on Thursday, stating that this novel strain is spreading quickly across certain regions of the nation. 
 South Africa, like other countries in the region, has experienced multiple waves of the COVID-19 pandemic.

President Biden's inauguration coincided with a critical gap in vaccine availability for American citizens.  States faced challenges obtaining both sufficient vaccine supplies and clear information regarding their allocation and timely delivery schedules. 
 Since assuming office, President Biden has overseen an extraordinary vaccination effort that has significantly improved the nation's COVID-19 situation.  In just 150 days, over 300 million shots have been administered nationwide. This surge in vaccinations has enabled the United States to witness a dramatic decline in daily fatalities and new infections. With these positive developments, the country has also begun to recover from the severe economic losses caused by the pandemic, with millions of jobs returning to the workforce.  
 Thanks to President [President's name]'s effective COVID-19 strategy, we are witnessing a decisive decline in the virus.  Over 300 million doses have already been administered within just 150 days, resulting in a dramatic reduction of more than 90 percent in both cases and deaths.  Simultaneously, the economy is experiencing the most robust rebound in decades, showcasing the far-reaching positive impact of this strategy. 
 The significant advancements in vaccination efforts have led to a dramatic decrease in COVID-19 cases, hospitalizations, and deaths. These figures are now at their lowest points since the beginning of the pandemic last year. 
 Since assuming office, the President has relentlessly spearheaded a comprehensive, national response to combat COVID-19— mobilizing every facet of government for wartime effort. This collaborative approach involved uniting public and private sector leaders alike to actively contribute towards shared goals. 
 If the U.S.'s vaccination pace of January had continued, reaching 300 million doses administered would have taken nearly three years—a staggering 336 days. Additionally, a lack of vaccine supply, qualified vaccinators, and readily available vaccination sites restricted Americans' access to these crucial shots. 
 President Biden's decisive actions ensured sufficient vaccine supply for all Americans, significantly boosted state allocations, and provided states with clear insight into distribution figures, enabling them to streamline vaccination efforts and accelerate the delivery of life-saving doses. 
 Over 15 states, along with Washington, D.C., have achieved vaccination coverage of 70% or more for their adult populations with just a single dose.  In addition, 26 states and D.C. have fully vaccinated at least half of their adult population.  
 Public health measures have significantly curbed the spread of the virus, saving tens of thousands of lives and allowing millions of Americans to resume normal activities. 
 Over 65% of American adults have received at least one dose of the vaccine, with 55% being fully vaccinated.  This trend is even more pronounced among seniors, as 87% of those aged 65 or older have been vaccinated, and  74% of adults aged 40 and over. 
 The current situation is directly attributable to the Biden administration's comprehensive approach, which treated this national challenge like a wartime situation.  This involved securing ample supplies for all Americans and rapidly distributing them into the hands of those who needed them most. 
 This early action enabled the President to rapidly expand vaccination eligibility to all adults by mid-April, exceeding initial expectations. This strategic move positions the United States as a key leader in global vaccine efforts to protect everyone worldwide. 
 This wasn't a random occurrence; it was a deliberate decision.  The President has vowed to meet this unprecedented challenge head-on, leveraging the full power and resources of the federal government. 
 Thanks to the Biden administration's comprehensive approach to creating a nationwide vaccination program, now over 175 million Americans have received at least one dose of the COVID-19 vaccine.  
 America is experiencing a resurgence of its identity, one that echoes with the spirit of summer joy and freedom. With over 300 million vaccinations administered within just 150 days, President Biden's efforts have propelled us toward a brighter future. In his inauguration, just 5% of adults were vaccinated. This progress marks a significant turning point in our national recovery.

Expanded over-the-counter (OTC) and point-of-care (POC) testing options provide numerous avenues for schools, workplaces, communities, and other organizations to implement effective serial screening programs.  These authorizations are in line with the Food and Drug Administration's (FDA) recent efforts to foster innovation in diagnostic test development. 
 The COVID-19 pandemic prompted us to collaborate with test developers to incorporate serial screening claims into their products.  To date, the FDA has authorized three tests capable of utilizing serial screening claims, enabling asymptomatic individuals to be tested repeatedly on a scheduled basis. 
 Screening COVID-19 testing, particularly with widely available at-home tests, plays a crucial role in national pandemic responses.  Numerous schools, workplaces, communities, and other organizations have established testing programs to swiftly identify cases of the virus. 
 "If a test is truly effective," stated Dr. Jeff Shuren, M.D., J.D., Director of the FDA's Center for Devices and Radiological Health, "we will authorize its use." 
 Over-the-counter (OTC) testing is permitted for various procedures, while a single serial screening test was granted authorization for use in point-of-care settings without a prescription, and an additional screening test was authorized for use at the point of care with a prescription. 
 The agency has authorized various over-the-counter (OTC) tests for serial screening, allowing these tests to be utilized without a prescription. 
 The FDA's authorization of multiple rigorous testing procedures provides assurance that these tests meet our scientific standards for emergency use authorization. 
 These authorizations are designed to facilitate the streamlined process for test developers seeking authorization for serially-conducted screenings, ultimately increasing consumer access to testing resources. This initiative complements the FDA's guidance materials that support schools, businesses, community organizations, and other entities in establishing effective screening programs. 
 This week, the following rapid diagnostic tests are authorized for over-the-counter (OTC) at-home use: Quidel QuickVue At-Home OTC COVID-19 test, Abbott BinaxNOW (multiple configurations), Abbott BinaxNOW COVID-19 Antigen Self Test, and Abbott BinaxNOW COVID-19 Ag Card 2 Home Test.  The Abbott BinaxNOW COVID-19 Ag Card 2 Home Test is also authorized for OTC at-home serial screening with telehealth proctoring. Additionally, the Abbott BinaxNOW COVID-19 Ag 2 Card is authorized for point-of-care (POC) serial screening without a prescription. Finally, the BD Veritor System for Rapid Detection of SARS-CoV-2 is authorized for POC serial screening with a prescription.

Previously, these tests were authorized by the agency to diagnose those experiencing COVID-19 symptoms. However, this week’s authorization allows asymptomatic individuals to be tested through serial testing. 
 The FDA played a crucial role in facilitating rapid testing development during the pandemic. We authorized tests swiftly, created multiple avenues for collaboration between developers and ourselves to bring approved tests to market, and issued clear enforcement policies for all COVID-19 tests. 
 The FDA continues its rapid progress in developing over-the-counter and other screening tests for COVID-19.  Following recent FDA actions aimed at accelerating testing development, the agency has moved swiftly to bring more readily accessible tests to market for asymptomatic individuals.

The Suez Canal's reopening on March 29th marks a crucial step toward restoring global economic stability.  Following its abrupt closure on March 23rd due to the grounding of the colossal container ship Ever Given, the canal’s re-opening serves as a potent reminder of the inherent fragility of the global economy in the face of extreme events.
 The Global Forecast System (GFS) indicates a significant windshift near the location where the Ever Given blockage occurred, with winds transitioning from southwest to west directionality. This shift was approximately five miles north of the ship's connection point to the Red Sea. 
 The Ever Given ran aground at 5:40 AM UTC on that day. 
 While there's currently no evidence suggesting climate change directly impacted the sandstorm causing the canal blockage, the incident sparks fascinating possibilities for further investigation into potential correlations. 
 A powerful combination of high winds and a raging sandstorm led to the grounding of the [vehicle name]. This meteorological phenomenon originated from a cold front that moved southwards, converging with a low-pressure system situated near southern Turkey. 
 On March 23rd, a cold front moved eastward across Egypt.  Winds, reaching speeds of 25-30 knots (approximately 29-35 mph), were channeled along the axis of the Red Sea, moving south-southeast and culminating at the southern end of the Suez Canal. This is illustrated in the GFS model's 6 UTC analysis (Figure 2). 
 A multi-day closure of a major shipping route highlights the fragility of our global food supply chains and economic stability. This vulnerability is exacerbated by escalating climate change impacts, including extreme weather events, rising sea levels, and the accelerating "just-in-time" logistics model used in international trade. 
 The Suez Canal Authority's initial explanation for the incident cited high winds and a sandstorm as contributing factors to reduced visibility, but later acknowledged that strong winds alone did not account for the incident. An ongoing investigation is currently underway to determine the full extent of the cause. 
 Figure 1 shows a MODIS satellite image from March 23, 2021, capturing the grounding of the container ship Ever Given in the Suez Canal. A powerful cold front sweeping across Egypt brought about widespread dust storms that impacted the Red Sea, Suez Canal, and waters surrounding the Mediterranean Sea.

Images of Earth provided by NASA's Earth Observatory, showcasing its beauty and dynamism through the lens of the Landsat program, as visualized and curated by Lauren Dauphin. 
 A canal authority official projected a three-day closure, while Refinitiv, a data company, estimated the impact could linger for ten days or longer.  The disruption has cost the canal an estimated $15 billion per day, according to Suez Canal Authority chairman Osama Rabie, as per reports from the BBC. 
 At a staggering height of 1,312 feet, the Ever Given rivals the Empire State Building’s impressive 1,454-foot stature, including its spire and antenna.  For a visual comparison, imagine the Ever Given standing among Manhattan’s iconic Central Park.  A composite 3D illustration provides an unprecedented glimpse of the vessel within the park's green expanse.  
 Powerful tugboats tirelessly navigate the waters, their job to expertly guide ships into port and facilitate movement between berths.  These specialized craft leverage their strength to maneuver vessels, either by direct physical contact or pulling using heavy tow lines. 
 The Mashhour is renowned as the world's largest and most potent cutter suction dredger, having been utilized extensively for widening and deepening sections of the Suez Canal during past projects. 
 The Ever Given has been successfully refloated and released from the Suez Canal!  Salvage teams used a combination of powerful tug boats, extensive dredging operations, and strategically timed high tides to free the massive container ship. This remarkable feat was accomplished on Monday. 
 The full moon coincides with heightened tides, known as spring tides or king tides, that significantly exceed average levels, according to the Associated Press.  Gravity's influence is strongest during an alignment of the Earth, moon, and sun in a straight line, further influencing tidal fluctuations. 
 To access submerged cargo, various methods are employed, including the use of bucket-like devices or powerful vacuum suction provided by hydraulic pumps. Notably, two dredgers played a pivotal role in refloating the container ship: the 10th of Ramadan and the Mashhour, both under the ownership of the Suez Canal Authority. 
 Workers unearthed 30,000 cubic meters of sand, enough to fill approximately a dozen Olympic-sized swimming pools, according to USA TODAY's report on Monday.  The vessel in question spans the length of a skyscraper. 
 The Ever Given container ship was successfully refloated from the Suez Canal on both Sunday and Monday.  Sunday saw the ship's stern freed from the canal's blockage, followed by the bow being removed Monday. According to the canal authority, the vessel emerged from the obstruction unharmed with no reported damage. 
 The higher-than-usual spring tides allowed for easier canal clearance.  Satellite imagery reveals that numerous ships are currently clustered around the Suez Canal, highlighting the significant backlog.

As Facebook pivots towards immersive technologies like virtual and augmented reality for work and communication, the ongoing challenges with their existing platforms raise a pertinent question: are these changes truly in line with their long-term vision? 
 Facebook is seemingly poised to revolutionize augmented reality with its cloud-connected AR glasses, and I was eager to see what they would deliver.  I put Facebook’s newest Ray-Ban smart glasses on my face, the photo-taking, audio-enabled sunglasses released last month – but I found myself hitting a wall. 
 Our dependence on technology is laid bare during power outages and network failures, reminding us of how intertwined our lives are with Google, smartphones, and our ever-connected social networks. 
 My Facebook app refused to connect, leaving the only way for my glasses to upload their pictures and videos inaccessible. This meant I was locked out of both my photos and access to those memories stored on the device. 
 Facebook's widespread outage crippled not just its core services but also its augmented and virtual reality (AR/VR) hardware, leaving users with a temporary visual impairment.  In the middle of the day-long blackout on Tuesday, I decided it was time to give VR a go. 
 App and cloud-connected devices often experience connectivity issues, regardless of the platform.  Experiencing disruptions with Facebook isn't an isolated issue; similar challenges can arise on other platforms like PlayStation 5 or Xbox Series X where network outages occur for services such as PlayStation Network or Xbox Live.  
 A wave of meme-filled tweets about people getting "trapped" in the metaverse had me pondering:  What if Facebook went offline?  Would my Oculus Quest still be functional, or would its magic vanish too? Even Facebook's newfangled camera-equipped sunglasses - were they truly independent of their parent company? 
 The Oculus Quest 2 offers a unique blend of standalone functionality and Facebook integration. While it operates primarily as a self-contained, Android-powered device for downloading and playing games, it also requires a Facebook login to unlock its full potential.  This creates a hybrid system that straddles the line between virtual reality and social media platforms. 
 My situation was a bizarre combination of odd and frustrating.  Despite the technical hiccup, my Oculus headset powered on and presented its usual virtual living room, followed by the familiar array of installed apps. 
 Numerous science fiction novels explore apocalyptic scenarios involving the collapse of infrastructure.  "Infinite Detail" by Tim Maughan offers an excellent introduction to this genre. 
 I started playing through Walkabout Mini Golf, chipping a few holes in before hopping into a quick online match against another player. Then, I jumped into Altspace VR for some relaxation.  Mid-session, I stumbled upon a meditation class, where avatars floated around as if nothing was amiss. 
 In a groundbreaking move by a rebellious community in the United Kingdom, they sought to dismantle a vast network of smart technology infrastructure. This act triggered a global system reset, pushing humanity towards a future where reliance on assisted technologies is no longer normalized, forcing them to adapt and rediscover life without their technological crutches. 
 The line blurs between our digital lives and real ones.  Facebook's interconnectedness, woven deeper into our experiences via the Oculus OS, might herald a new reality: an evolving "metaverse" where Facebook applications become our sole access point for daily life. What does this mean for the future of apps as we know them? 
 While certain functions of Facebook's glasses might function perfectly, others likely will not.  These glasses can capture photos and videos without an internet connection, but they require the corresponding smartphone app to share them. 
 Facebook was completely inaccessible.  There was no app store, no avatars, and no way to connect with friends, even the app icon wouldn't load.  However, I still had the ability to play the games I had already downloaded. 
 Many online services seem unreliable at times, with some functioning perfectly while others experience outages.  Streaming platforms like Netflix and Disney+ may be a viable alternative during these periods of downtime. With so many aspects of our daily life now reliant on cloud-based services, including always-on wearables, we've become accustomed to placing our lives in the hands of technology. 
 The future of augmented and virtual reality (AR/VR) needn’t be limited by reliance on a constant internet connection.  Existing hardware offers the potential for on-device processing, enabling VR experiences to run offline and AR effects to function without online connectivity.

Imagine BGP as an intricate map system, guiding internet traffic to websites with the most efficient routes.  While this mapping process aims for speed, real-world scenarios often reveal that "shortest" doesn't necessarily equate to "best". 
 Several reports attribute the incident to BGP, the Border Gateway Protocol, citing internal Facebook sources, traffic analysis, and common intuition suggesting a connection to DNS or BGP. 
 The physical landscape of real roads rarely shifts dramatically, but online platforms are constantly evolving.  Websites might relocate their servers across international borders, adjust existing services, or even entirely alter how they operate, all while navigating the complexities of an ever-changing internet ecosystem. 
 Facebook's services are now restored, with the company offering an explanation for the recent outages.  While they claim BGP played a role in the problems (arguing it functioned as expected), this has sparked another question: what exactly is BGP? 
 Steering left toward the river is a simple choice, but even with well-charted maps, unforeseen detours are possible.  After all, remember the early days of GPS when every trip felt like an impromptu wilderness adventure? Jokes abounded about navigating into the wrong landscape – off a cliff or straight into the middle of a desert! 
 Autonomous systems collaborate by sharing their maps.  They periodically communicate with neighboring islands to receive and incorporate any updated information. 
 With the internet constantly evolving, map accuracy is paramount.  You wouldn't want your internet service provider to direct you on a route that's become obsolete and doesn't reach Google. 
 Like in other network configurations, mistakes in Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) can unintentionally route traffic to unintended destinations, potentially causing disruptions.  Uncaught errors can also permanently alter routing tables, impacting the connectivity and reliability of networks worldwide. 
 Building bridges between every single island would present a monumental engineering challenge.  This is where Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) steps in; it acts as a guide, indicating the optimal routes and connections required to reach your desired location from any specific island or autonomous system. 
 I recall studying graph theory in my algorithms and data structures course, where we attempted to construct algorithms for finding the shortest path.  I agree with you there.  As soon as I encountered graphs, I decided to pursue other paths. 
 There are several potential pitfalls, but we'll delve into those later.  Let's start with an example: Imagine you want to access a fictional tech news website called Convergence. How might this connection go wrong? 
 Networks often resemble island nations; their connections are centralized and managed by a single governing power. This power can take the form of an ISP such as Comcast, a private corporation like Facebook, or even a large organization like a government agency or university. 
 Think of Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) like a network of mapmakers constantly updating instructions for getting to websites like YouTube or Facebook. 
 BGP (Border Gateway Protocol) is a core routing protocol used by internet service providers (ISPs) to exchange routing information between networks.  This protocol played a critical role in Facebook's recent massive outage. When Facebook experienced a widespread outage impacting its network, BGP contributed significantly to the wider effects seen across various services like Instagram and WhatsApp, highlighting the intricate interconnectivity within the digital world. 
 If the only available path is BGP, it will facilitate communication between you and Convergence. However, if alternative routes are possible – such as connecting DecadeConnect and NetSend to ThirdLevel – BGP may prefer to use that direct connection due to its shorter hop distance. 
 BGP (Border Gateway Protocol) is a critical system that helps deliver your internet traffic efficiently and quickly around the world.  
 BGP's role is to navigate networks, ensuring they find the optimal path.  I've often compared it to a postal service, an air traffic control system, or even a map.  Its intricate workings make it truly unique. 
 Convergence relies on ISP NetSend and your specific connection, DecadeConnect.  NetSend and DecadeConnect cannot communicate directly, but your ISP can bridge the communication gap by talking to Border Communications, who then connects with Form, which finally communicates with NetSend. 
 BGP acts like a navigation system, guiding your computer's route to Facebook. Think of it this way: the internet is organized into smaller regions called autonomous systems (AS), just like a map with cities and towns, but for internet traffic. 
 With numerous ISPs, backbone routers, and servers involved in delivering data to platforms like Facebook, countless potential routes exist for your packets to take. 
 Routing algorithms often prioritize paths based on various factors, including cost and network-specific access fees.  Some networks charge for the inclusion of specific routes within their infrastructure. 
 Multiple roadways could have entirely different names on different maps—a stark contrast when you consider a town with just five main streets.  Trying to tie the world's internet together would be like trying to find a single thread in a tapestry woven from countless strands! 
 Mapping unchanging infrastructure, like roads, can be challenging enough.  But imagine attempting to chart the ever-evolving landscape of the internet itself - it's an even greater feat! I recently encountered this firsthand while planning a trip where road networks differed depending on the map being used.

Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and WhatsApp experienced widespread service disruptions affecting a portion of users Wednesday afternoon.  Users reported intermittent outages for all four platforms shortly after 2 p.m. ET. 
 According to DownDetector.com, issues with WhatsApp began on Wednesday evening.  Similarly, Meta, now the parent company of Facebook, confirmed problems with Messenger, Workplace Chat, and Instagram Direct Messages in a statement to USA TODAY. 
 Most users were unaffected by the outage by around 6 pm ET, according to DownDetector.com.  Later, at approximately 7:20 pm ET, Meta issued a statement to USA TODAY: Earlier today, some individuals encountered issues accessing Workplace Chat, Instagram Direct Messages, and Messenger.
